# Phase 10.0.9 Dependency Sovereignty Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, autonomous migration systems, automated provider-switching systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve constitutional sovereignty, vendor survivability, infrastructure independence, portability, and dependency resilience after ethical survivability and anti-corruption doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional dependency-sovereignty doctrine.
- Vendor survivability philosophy.
- Infrastructure independence architecture.
- Anti-dependency-capture doctrine.
- External-provider survivability standards.
- Constitutional portability philosophy.
- Dependency concentration containment framework.
- Civilization infrastructure sovereignty doctrine.
- Governance independence survivability architecture.
- Constitutional migration survivability doctrine.
- Phase 10.0.9 dependency sovereignty summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No dependency-governance layer creates runtime authority.
- No portability review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No migration survivability layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No dependency-sovereignty framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Dependency sovereignty remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, and non-automatic.
- Portability is preservation of meaning and evidence, not automated migration.
- Vendor survivability is continuity doctrine, not provider onboarding or provider switching.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, autonomous migration systems, automated provider-switching systems, or execution systems.
